title: Trip Planner - Architecture & Code Patterns
summary: Architectural reference describing named code patterns, LangChain integration, error handling, testing strategy, embedded ADRs, tech stack, and project structure for the Trip Planner backend and frontend.
notes: Dominantly a documentation/reference guide (prose + patterns + tech stack + project structure), but contains five embedded ADR sections (ADR-001 through ADR-005) with explicit Status fields. ADR-001, ADR-003, ADR-004, ADR-005 are 'Accepted' (locked decisions); ADR-002 is 'Deprecated'. Synthesizer should consider extracting these embedded ADRs as locked decisions even though the parent doc is classified DOC. Type ambiguity: DOC vs ADR-collection — chose DOC because the document is primarily an architecture guide with ADRs as one of many sections, not a single decision record.
